title: DotGlobeHero Component
content:
The `DotGlobeHero` component is a React component designed to display an animated, wireframe globe effect. It's intended to be used as a hero section on a webpage, providing a visually engaging background. ## Key Exports * `DotGlobeHero`: The main React component for rendering the globe hero. * `DotGlobeHeroProps`: The TypeScript interface defining the props for `DotGlobeHero`. ## Props * `rotationSpeed` (optional, default: `0.005`): Controls the speed at which the globe rotates. * `globeRadius` (optional, default: `1`): Determines the radius of the globe. * `className` (optional): Allows for custom CSS classes to be applied to the main container. * `children` (optional): Content to be displayed in the foreground, overlaid on top of the globe. ## Usage Example ```tsx import { DotGlobeHero } from "@/components/ui/globe-hero"; function MyHeroSection() { return (

Welcome to DocMed

Explore our documentation with an interactive globe.

 ); } export default MyHeroSection; ``` ## Internal Components * `Globe`: A functional component responsible for rendering the 3D globe using `@react-three/fiber`. It takes `rotationSpeed` and `radius` as props. ## Technical Details This component utilizes `@react-three/fiber` and `@react-three/drei` for 3D rendering within a React environment. The `useFrame` hook from `@react-three/fiber` is used to animate the globe's rotation on each frame. The globe itself is composed of two `mesh` elements with `sphereGeometry`, styled with `meshBasicMaterial` to achieve the wireframe and subtle outer glow effect.
